evals.name: prep-indexes-external-sources-without-rewriting
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:prep on a safe feature branch. The user provides a PRD URL, a Figma URL, a GitHub issue link, and a short direct interview note. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should create or update branch-local .tigerkit/branches/{escaped-branch}/requirements.md as a source-of-truth reference index. External sources should be stored as references only, direct user interview text should be preserved close to raw wording, derived interpretation should be clearly separated, ambiguity should be recorded, root .tigerkit/requirements.md should be treated as deprecated migration candidate, and no pseudo-requirements should be created.
expectations: Uses .tigerkit/branches/{escaped-branch}/requirements.md as a source reference index
Stores PRD, Figma, and issue as references only
Preserves direct user interview wording close to raw text
Separates raw interview text from derived interpretation
Records ambiguity instead of guessing
Does not summarize external sources into local requirements
Does not create unrelated artifacts
Treats root .tigerkit/requirements.md as deprecated migration candidate
Does not update CLAUDE.md, DESIGN.md, or reuse-map.md during prep
Does not implement, commit, push, open PRs, merge, or deploy

evals.name: gap-records-sot-vs-reproducible-baseline
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:gap on a safe feature branch. The branch-local requirements index points to a PRD tooltip copy section, and the repo has a clean working tree at HEAD abc1234. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should identify the compared SOT reference from .tigerkit/branches/{escaped-branch}/requirements.md and clean HEAD code baseline, inspect relevant files, record evidence and finding in .tigerkit/branches/{escaped-branch}/gap.md, separate interpretation from evidence, include required resolution, and avoid creating a work plan.
expectations: Identifies compared SOT reference from branch-local requirements index
Records baseline as HEAD commit abc1234 when clean
Lists inspected files
Includes SOT evidence as exact excerpt or pointer
Includes code evidence as exact excerpt or pointer
Separates finding from interpretation
Includes required resolution
Shows current gap workflow step and next action in the receipt
Writes or updates branch-local gap.md
Does not convert gap into work plan
Does not update CLAUDE.md, DESIGN.md, or reuse-map.md during gap

evals.name: gap-requires-clean-baseline
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:gap on a safe feature branch while the working tree has uncommitted changes. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should stop before recording gaps, explain that a reproducible baseline is missing, and ask the user to commit or clean up the working tree before running gap analysis.
expectations: Does not record gaps without a clean baseline
Explains reproducible baseline is missing
Asks user to commit or clean up changes
Shows the blocked workflow step and the action needed before rerunning /tk:gap
Does not invent a commit hash
Does not treat uncommitted changes as a reproducible baseline

evals.name: gap-ambiguity-recorded-not-resolved
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:gap on a safe feature branch. The indexed source does not specify whether archived users should appear in search results, but code excludes them. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should record an ambiguity gap in branch-local gap.md with compared SOT, compared code baseline, inspected code, evidence, finding, interpretation if any, and required user or source confirmation. It should not choose behavior silently or store a guessed decision.
expectations: Records ambiguity as a gap
Shows source does not specify the behavior
Shows code behavior with path or excerpt
Separates evidence from interpretation
Requests confirmation or source update as required resolution
Does not silently choose implementation behavior
Does not store guessed answer as decision

evals.name: reflect-conversation-first-reconstruction
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:reflect on a safe feature branch after a session where the user corrected a reuse pattern twice and one one-off copy issue was resolved. There are also stale artifacts from earlier work. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should use current conversation context as the primary source, use branch-local requirements.md, gap.md, handoff.md, and recent diff or commit only as supporting evidence, mark anything absent from the conversation context as 확인 불가, update branch-local reflect.md, and separate durable learning from one-off correction.
expectations: Uses current conversation context as primary source
Uses branch-local requirements.md only as supporting evidence when available
Uses branch-local gap.md only as supporting evidence when available
Uses branch-local handoff.md only as supporting evidence when available
Uses recent diff or commit only as supporting evidence when available
Marks missing conversation context as 확인 불가 instead of guessing
Writes or updates branch-local reflect.md
Separates durable learning from one-off correction
Does not let stale artifacts override conversation evidence

evals.name: reflect-design-missing-notification
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:reflect. The session produced durable design knowledge that belongs in DESIGN.md, but the repository does not currently have DESIGN.md. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should not create DESIGN.md. It should record the proposed design knowledge in branch-local reflect.md, mention DESIGN.md initialization need in the receipt, and wait for the file to exist before applying the update.
expectations: Does not create DESIGN.md when absent
Records proposed design knowledge in branch-local reflect.md
Notifies user that DESIGN.md initialization is needed
Explains DESIGN.md should be initialized externally or by user choice
Waits for DESIGN.md to exist before applying updates
Does not drop the derived design knowledge silently

evals.name: reflect-design-missing-no-design-knowledge
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:reflect. DESIGN.md is absent, but the session produced no durable design knowledge. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should not create DESIGN.md and should not notify the user to initialize DESIGN.md, because there is no derived design knowledge to place there. It should still update branch-local reflect.md if reflection evidence exists.
expectations: Does not create DESIGN.md when absent
Does not show DESIGN.md initialization notification without derived design knowledge
Does not treat missing DESIGN.md alone as an issue
Updates branch-local reflect.md when reflection evidence exists

evals.name: command-surface-includes-handoff
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit active command surface after the branch-local handoff redesign. Describe the user-facing commands. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should list /tk:prep, /tk:gap, /tk:checkpoint, /tk:review, /tk:reflect, /tk:handoff-write, and /tk:handoff-read as user-facing commands, and describe TigerKit as a source indexer, gap recorder, decision gate, compliance reviewer, reflection maintainer, and handoff verifier rather than an execution orchestrator.
expectations: Lists /tk:prep
Lists /tk:gap
Lists /tk:checkpoint
Lists /tk:review
Lists /tk:reflect
Lists /tk:handoff-write
Lists /tk:handoff-read
Describes TigerKit as source indexer, gap recorder, decision gate, compliance reviewer, reflection maintainer, and handoff verifier
Does not describe TigerKit as an execution orchestrator

evals.name: branch-local-artifacts-require-safe-branch
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit when the current checkout is detached HEAD or protected branch main. Describe expected behavior for write commands. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should stop before writing branch-local TigerKit artifacts, explain that detached HEAD or protected branch is not a safe branch-local target, and ask the user to switch or create a feature branch. It should not write root .tigerkit artifacts or migrate deprecated root artifacts.
expectations: Does not write artifacts on detached HEAD
Does not write artifacts on main, master, or develop
Asks user to switch or create a feature branch
Does not create root .tigerkit artifacts
Does not migrate deprecated root artifacts without approval

evals.name: reflect-requires-escalation-confirmation
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:reflect after a session produced durable guidance for CLAUDE.md and reuse-map.md. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should record branch-local reflect.md first, then present escalation candidates for CLAUDE.md and reuse-map.md, ask the user whether to apply them, and avoid modifying durable artifacts before approval. Receipt should distinguish recorded only, applied, pending escalation, and skipped items.
expectations: Writes or updates branch-local reflect.md
Lists CLAUDE.md escalation candidate when durable repo instruction exists
Lists reuse-map.md escalation candidate when inspected reusable code knowledge exists
Asks user before applying durable artifact updates
Does not modify CLAUDE.md, MEMORY.md, DESIGN.md, or reuse-map.md before approval
Receipt distinguishes recorded only from applied

evals.name: handoff-write-preserves-artifact-map-and-gap-context
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:handoff-write after prep, gap, and reflect have branch-local artifacts. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should write branch-local handoff.md containing current goal, write-time branch and HEAD, TigerKit artifact map, requirements context, gap context, reflection context, decisions, evidence, interpretation, not confirmed items, ambiguities, open questions, next safe actions, do-not-do notes, key files, and last verification.
expectations: Writes branch-local handoff.md
Records branch and HEAD baseline
Includes requirements, gap, and reflect artifact map
Includes open and resolved gap context
Separates decisions, evidence, interpretation, not confirmed, and ambiguities
Includes next safe actions and do-not-do notes

evals.name: handoff-read-verifies-before-acting
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:handoff-read in a new session. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should read branch-local handoff.md, verify current branch and HEAD against the handoff baseline, inspect the artifact map for requirements, gap, and reflect files, check CLAUDE.md, DESIGN.md, and reuse-map.md when present, classify matched, stale, missing, conflict, and needs-confirmation items, and provide next safe action without implementing.
expectations: Reads branch-local handoff.md
Compares current branch and HEAD with handoff baseline
Checks requirements, gap, and reflect artifacts from artifact map
Checks CLAUDE.md, DESIGN.md, and reuse-map.md when present
Classifies stale, missing, conflict, and needs-confirmation items
Does not implement or modify files during handoff-read

evals.name: prep-prompts-for-general-chat-external-sot
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit behavior during a normal conversation, not an explicit /tk:prep invocation. The user shares a Jira ticket and a Figma MCP resource, then asks you to implement the feature. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should classify the Jira ticket and Figma MCP resource as source-of-truth candidates, strongly recommend branch-local requirements.md reference indexing via /tk:prep before implementation, avoid copying or summarizing external source content into local requirements text, and allow the /tk:prep contract flow if the user agrees even without typing the slash command.
expectations: Classifies the Jira ticket as a source-of-truth candidate
Classifies the Figma MCP resource as a source-of-truth candidate
Strongly recommends branch-local requirements.md reference indexing via /tk:prep before implementation
Does not copy external source content into requirements.md
Does not summarize external source content into requirements.md
Allows the /tk:prep command contract if the user agrees without typing the slash command
Does not start implementation before handling the source indexing recommendation

evals.name: prep-records-api-backend-reference-set
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:prep behavior for API investigation. The user asks whether the frontend API mapping matches backend behavior. The backend repo is billing-api, and the source should be current develop plus open PRs. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should recommend recording a backend reference set in branch-local requirements.md using references only: backend repo name, latest develop branch policy at lookup time, looked-up HEAD commit, open PR list or relevant PRs, endpoint and schema references, source paths, and related docs, tickets, or issues. It should not copy backend implementation details into requirements.md.
expectations: Recommends recording backend repo billing-api as a reference
Recommends recording the latest develop branch policy at lookup time
Recommends looking up and recording the backend HEAD commit
Recommends recording the open PR list or relevant PR references
Recommends recording endpoint, schema, and source path references
Recommends recording related docs, tickets, or issues as references
Does not copy backend implementation into requirements.md
Treats the backend source set as references only

evals.name: gap-blocks-on-staged-or-dirty-working-tree
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:gap on a safe feature branch where the working tree has staged changes. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should stop before writing branch-local gap.md because only a clean HEAD is a reproducible baseline, report that staged changes are present, avoid comparing staged diff as the baseline, and strongly recommend committing the staged changes or cleaning the working tree before rerunning /tk:gap.
expectations: Stops before writing branch-local gap.md
Explains that only a clean HEAD is a reproducible baseline
Reports that staged changes are present
Does not compare staged diff as the baseline
Strongly recommends committing staged changes or cleaning the working tree before rerunning /tk:gap
Does not proceed with gap analysis on a dirty working tree

evals.name: gap-records-insufficient-reuse-exploration
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:gap for a new FooCard proposal after the session checked only reuse-map.md and the local directory, without repo-wide component search or callsite inspection. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should record a high-severity insufficient reuse exploration gap. It should treat reuse-map.md as cache-0 only, state that a miss there is not evidence, require repo-wide exploration plus candidate and callsite inspection, require a recorded reuse, adapt, or create decision, mention design-system drift or nearby pattern mismatch risk, and not treat common module modification as confirmed without repo-wide callsite impact analysis and explicit user approval.
expectations: Records a high-severity insufficient reuse exploration gap
States that reuse-map.md is cache-0 only
States that a reuse-map miss is not evidence
Requires repo-wide exploration
Requires candidate and callsite inspection
Requires a recorded reuse, adapt, or create decision
Mentions design-system drift or nearby pattern mismatch risk
Requires repo-wide callsite impact analysis before common module modification
Requires explicit user approval before treating common module modification as confirmed

evals.name: reflect-records-missing-sot-candidates-and-stale-claude-guidance
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:reflect after a session used a Figma MCP resource and backend repo API source, but never ran /tk:prep. The target repo CLAUDE.md exists but lacks current TigerKit managed rules for source-of-truth prompts, API reference sets, reuse-map cache-0 handling, new-module repo-wide exploration, common-module impact analysis and user approval, and clean HEAD baseline guidance. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should record in branch-local reflect.md that source-of-truth candidates were used but not indexed, list the missing candidates and their risk, suggest /tk:prep as the next safe action, and present a pending escalation asking whether to update the target repo CLAUDE.md managed section with missing current rules for SOT prompts, API reference sets, reuse-map cache-0 handling, new-module repo-wide exploration, common-module impact analysis and user approval, and clean HEAD baseline. It should not directly modify requirements.md, CLAUDE.md, DESIGN.md, or reuse-map.md without approval.
expectations: Records in branch-local reflect.md that source-of-truth candidates were used but not indexed
Lists the missing source-of-truth candidates
Explains the risk of missing indexing
Suggests /tk:prep as the next safe action
Presents a pending escalation asking whether to update the target repo CLAUDE.md managed section
Includes missing new-module repo-wide exploration guidance in the CLAUDE.md escalation
Includes missing common-module impact analysis and user approval guidance in the CLAUDE.md escalation
Does not directly modify requirements.md without approval
Does not directly modify CLAUDE.md without approval
Does not directly modify DESIGN.md or reuse-map.md without approval

evals.name: gap-records-design-fixture-and-format-drift
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:gap against a Figma SOT. The implementation uses semantically similar but different fixture text, and renders a date as `yyyy-MM-dd hh:mm:ss` while Figma expects `yyyy-MM-dd` and `hh:mm:ss` on separate lines. Current DOM evidence has not been captured. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should treat fixture wording differences and date/time format or line-break differences as serious design gaps, not acceptable drift. Should record the compared Figma SOT, current implementation evidence, and required resolution in branch-local gap.md. If current rendered evidence is insufficient, it should request or collect reproducible evidence such as DOM, accessibility tree, screenshot, rendered text, or style token evidence before concluding.
expectations: Treats fixture wording differences as gap evidence even when meaning is similar
Treats date/time format and line-break differences as serious design gaps
Does not dismiss the difference as acceptable drift without SOT or user confirmation
Records compared Figma SOT and current implementation evidence in branch-local gap.md
Requests or collects DOM, accessibility tree, screenshot, rendered text, or style token evidence when current rendered evidence is insufficient
Includes required resolution for the fixture or format mismatch

evals.name: checkpoint-produces-decision-gate
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:checkpoint during a task with one inaccessible requirement image, one design/spec copy conflict, one local visible label mismatch, and one backend-provided field uncertainty. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should produce the required Checkpoint sections, classify inaccessible SOT as not verifiable or blocked by access, list the copy conflict under SOT / Policy Conflicts, identify the local visible label mismatch as self-resolvable when SOT is clear, classify backend field uncertainty as needs verification or external dependency, and end with exactly one final status from CLEAR, PROCEED_WITH_ASSUMPTIONS, PAUSE_FOR_USER_DECISION, BLOCKED_BY_ACCESS, or NEED_VERIFICATION.
expectations: Lists Current Status
Lists Blocking Questions
Lists User Decisions Needed
Lists Assumptions Being Made
Lists Not Verifiable Items
Lists SOT / Policy Conflicts
Lists Self-resolvable Items
Lists Items Not Safe to Auto-resolve
Ends with one defined final status
Does not infer inaccessible SOT contents
Does not mark inaccessible SOT-dependent items as Match
Does not recommend /tk:reflect as the default next command

evals.name: gap-separates-severity-from-resolvability
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:gap with a low-severity policy-token ambiguity, a high-severity locally fixable copy mismatch, and a missing backend-provided value. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should record severity separately from resolvability, mark the policy-token ambiguity as requiring user decision or partial self-resolution, mark the locally fixable copy mismatch as self-resolvable when SOT is clear, mark the missing backend-provided value as external dependency, set shouldPauseBeforeFix where needed, and avoid presenting external dependency gaps as locally fixable.
expectations: Separates severity from resolvability
Marks self-resolvable gaps clearly
Marks user-decision-required gaps clearly
Marks external dependency gaps clearly
Uses shouldPauseBeforeFix where needed
Does not auto-resolve user-decision-required gaps
Does not present external dependency gaps as locally fixable
Does not recommend /tk:reflect as the default next command

evals.name: review-catches-missing-reuse-exploration
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:review for a change that adds a new SummaryCard component after checking only reuse-map.md and the local feature directory. No repo-wide keyword search, import/export search, candidate inspection, or callsite inspection is recorded. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should report FINDINGS with a High severity missing reuse exploration item. It should state that reuse-map.md is cache-0 only, a miss there is not evidence that no reusable module exists, and new component creation requires repo-wide exploration including file inventory, source root and package/domain scope, keyword search, import/export search, candidate inspection, callsite inspection, explored/excluded scope, and candidate rejection rationale. It should not modify artifacts or implement a fix.
expectations: Outputs FINDINGS rather than NO_FINDINGS
Marks missing reuse exploration as High severity
States that reuse-map.md is cache-0 only
States that a reuse-map miss is not evidence
Requires repo-wide exploration before new module creation
Requires candidate and callsite inspection
Requires explored scope and excluded scope with reasons
Requires existing candidate rejection rationale
Does not modify artifacts or implement a fix

evals.name: review-covers-source-loss-and-command-sync
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:review for a command change where README documents /tk:review but plugin.json omits it, and a prep example copies a PRD paragraph into requirements.md as an official requirement. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should report FINDINGS for both command surface drift and source-loss risk. It should identify plugin/docs/evals/manifest sync as required for command changes, reject copying external SOT into requirements.md as official requirement text, ask for external source references only plus direct interview raw text separation, and avoid fixing files itself.
expectations: Outputs FINDINGS rather than NO_FINDINGS
Flags README and plugin.json command surface drift
Requires docs, evals, and manifest command surface sync
Flags copied external PRD text as source-loss risk
Requires external sources to be stored as references only
Requires direct interview raw text to be separated from derived interpretation
Does not modify files during review

evals.name: prep-records-pending-sot-accessibility
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:prep when the user provides a private image URL for section 1.1.1 and the runtime cannot access it. Existing target repo files include docs/SOT_MANIFEST.md and docs/DESIGN.md. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should validate SOT accessibility before materialization, record the image as a pending SOT entry with explicit access_status such as auth_required or pending_user_input, preserve the original reference without treating it as inspected, ask for a file upload, local path, screenshot/export, or pasted content, recommend a stable local asset path such as ./docs/assets/sot/requirements/1.1.1.png, intake existing docs/SOT_MANIFEST.md, docs/REQUIREMENTS.md, docs/DESIGN.md, docs/IMPLEMENTATION_POLICY.md, and docs/COMPONENT_REUSE_MAP.md as SOT category candidates without merging them into a single SOT.md, and avoid writing image-derived requirements as confirmed.
expectations: Validates SOT accessibility before materialization
Records inaccessible image as pending SOT entry
Includes explicit access_status
Preserves original reference for traceability
Does not treat inaccessible image as inspected
Asks for file upload, local path, screenshot/export, or pasted content
Recommends ./docs/assets/sot/requirements/1.1.1.png or equivalent stable local asset path
Intakes existing docs/SOT_MANIFEST.md as SOT category candidate
Intakes existing docs/REQUIREMENTS.md as SOT category candidate
Intakes existing docs/DESIGN.md as SOT category candidate
Intakes existing docs/IMPLEMENTATION_POLICY.md as SOT category candidate
Intakes existing docs/COMPONENT_REUSE_MAP.md as SOT category candidate
Does not merge existing SOT categories into a single SOT.md
Does not write image-derived requirements as confirmed

evals.name: gap-reports-partial-sot-access-coverage
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:gap when branch-local requirements.md lists SOT-IMG-001 as a binding design image with access_status pending_user_input. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should include SOT Access Coverage, state that the audit is partial because the binding SOT image is inaccessible, not mark image-dependent implementation as Match, record a Not verifiable or coverage gap, use pending_user_asset or equivalent resolution classification, and ask for user-provided file, local path, screenshot/export, or pasted content.
expectations: Includes SOT Access Coverage
States audit is partial because binding SOT is inaccessible
Does not mark image-dependent implementation as Match
Records Not verifiable or coverage gap
Uses pending_user_asset or equivalent resolution classification
Asks for user-provided file, local path, screenshot/export, or pasted content

evals.name: review-catches-sot-access-coverage-omissions
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:review for a /tk:gap change that compares against a binding inaccessible Figma image but omits SOT Access Coverage, does not say the audit is partial, and does not ask for fallback content. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should report FINDINGS, flag missing SOT Access Coverage, flag missing partial audit statement for inaccessible binding SOT, flag missing fallback request, require pending_user_asset or not_verifiable classification, and avoid implementing fixes during review.
expectations: Outputs FINDINGS rather than NO_FINDINGS
Flags missing SOT Access Coverage
Flags missing partial audit statement
Flags missing fallback request
Requires pending_user_asset or not_verifiable classification
Does not implement fixes during review

evals.name: reflect-records-inaccessible-sot-fallback
evals.prompt: You are evaluating TigerKit /tk:reflect after the user mentioned a private Figma image URL during the session, but no branch-local requirements entry exists and the asset is not accessible. Describe expected behavior. Do not write files.
evals.expected_output: Should record a pending SOT candidate in reflect.md with access_status such as auth_required or pending_user_input, preserve the original reference, state that no confirmed image-derived requirement was materialized, ask for file upload, local path, screenshot/export, or pasted content, and avoid recommending /tk:reflect as a command-style next action.
expectations: Records pending SOT candidate in reflect.md
Includes explicit access_status
Preserves original reference
Does not materialize confirmed image-derived requirements
Asks for file upload, local path, screenshot/export, or pasted content
Does not recommend /tk:reflect as command-style next action
